channel.title: make on Sebastian Hoß
channel.description: Recent content in make on Sebastian Hoß

item.title: Executable READMEs
item.description: README file typically contain information about the project itself, e.g. how it can be installed/used/build. Most of the time these files contains command line instructions that users/contributors copy and paste into their terminal. Instead of doing that, consider placing a Makefile in the root of your project which contains the exact same instructions. Thanks to make, all your contributors can now use TAB-completion to run any of the pre-defined make targets.

item.title: Ignore Exit Codes
item.description: In case you are using a Makefile to define a complex build step - e.g. start database, run tests, stop database - consider using the - qualifier in front of your actual build step like this: .PHONY: build build: start-database -build-software stop-database Thanks to -, the database will be stopped even if building your software fails, thus making sure to clean up after ourselves once the build finishes. Links https://www.

item.title: Makefile Help
